notes_internal: Age was documented as a median range.

Samples were collected between June to October 2021 and from June to October 2022. Molecular analysis based on PCR-RFLP took place between January to June 2023.
Data was also not completely broken down by geographical location, therefore it was summarized

The title in the extraction article was changed from "Parasitological and Clinical Effects of Malaria Chemoprevention with Sulfadoxine-Pyrimethamine in Pregnant Women from Southern Mozambique: Malaria Chemoprevention and Resistance" to present title.

Data extracted from prevalence of mutants on fig 5b and 6b. 
Excluded mixed samples.
Haplotype prevalence reported for sum of sites whereas individual SNP prevalence was reported for individual sites

To calculate the K13 wildtype, calculate the mean of the sample sizes. Then calculate the 
sum of prevalences. Subtract the sum of prevalences from the mean samples size to get 
the number of samples with the wildtype (present).
Note that the K13 sample size of wildtype and SNPs are different, hence could not be displayed on the Surveyor with current algortihm.

notes: The two mutations K189T and K248R were identified but we did not record them because they were outside of the propeller region.

notes_internal: Data for AL and AQ groups was combined.
Point mutation at PfK13 gene was observed, but no prevalance data was provided.
Extracted all other SNPs except pfk13
Only Western Kenya data was recorded because of insufficient information from other sites.
The data for the seven provinces/sites were combined.
Only pregnant women included in the study
Targeted Codon specific sequencing for pfk13
